sanskrit:
शौर्यं तेजो धृतिर्दाक्ष्यं युद्धे चाप्यपलायनम्।
दानमीश्वरभावश्च क्षात्रं कर्म स्वभावजम्॥
transliteration: śauryaṃ tejo dhṛtir dākṣyaṃ yuddhe cāpy apalāyanam | dānam īśvara-bhāvaś ca kṣātraṃ karma svabhāva-jam ||
literal_meaning: Heroism, vigor, firmness, skill, not fleeing from battle, generosity, and the disposition of a ruler—these are the kṣatriya’s duties born of his own nature.
contextual_meaning: Krishna lists the innate psychological traits of the protector/leader temperament: courage, vitality, resilience, strategic competence, non-avoidance under threat, benevolent use of resources, and the instinct to take charge.
psychological_state: Integrated assertiveness—ego-syntonic alignment with the warrior role; capacity to face conflict without dissociation or paralysis.
trigger_scenario: A frontline nurse in a pandemic who feels compelled to volunteer for the ICU despite personal risk, experiences surge of energy (tejas), refuses to abandon patients (apalāyanam), and naturally coordinates the team (īśvara-bhāva).
anchor_text: Lead with courage, skill, and generosity when duty calls.
